title: Lifecycle convergence — demo-up auto set-dress + cold-start capture. /demo-up now auto-set-dresses by default (cache-first snapshot replay -> small-200 light seed) at the bring-up tail, EXACTLY as /dev-up has since M13, by REUSING the same dev-setdress.sh engine via --stack-type demo (one engine, two lifecycles). Plus the net-new corpus/ops/snapshot-cold-start.md runbook (the fresh-box capture workflow + why the wired postgres MCP is NOT a capture source). The closing milestone of v1.3b.
position_note: M20 is the 5th + FINAL milestone of v1.3b 'dress rehearsal' (M16->M20). It closes the dev<->demo asymmetry (ISSUE-10: dev-up auto-set-dressed since M13, demo-up didn't) and unblocks the real catalog on a fresh box (ISSUE-13). Depends on M18 (the post-set-dress verify) + M19 (the full experience) + M17 (re-run-safe primitives make auto-chaining safe to retry). After this close: /developer-kit:close-release v1.3b.
kind: a tooling change in rosetta-extensions (the set-dress chaining in demo-stack/up-injected.sh reusing the M13 dev-setdress.sh pass, made stack-type-aware via --stack-type dev|demo) + docs in rosetta (the net-new cold-start runbook + safety.md §2.7 + demo recipe/skill updates). The testable logic lives in the SEPARATE nested .agentspace/rosetta-extensions repo (bash: dev-stack/dev-setdress.sh stack-type/preset/atomicity, demo-stack/up-injected.sh the M20 chain). The rosetta m20/lifecycle-convergence branch carries only corpus/ops/snapshot-cold-start.md (net-new) + safety.md §2.7 + the demo README/recipe + the demo-up SKILL + cross-links (db-access/snapshot-spec/CLAUDE.md) + the milestone tracking docs.

tests.language: python (stdlib unittest, driving the bash via subprocess + CLI stubs on PATH) + shellcheck/py_compile on the 2 touched bash scripts + markdown (the net-new cold-start runbook + the safety/recipe/skill edits = the rosetta deliverable). No Go touched. The safety.md §2.7 edit is pinned by the M15 Go drift guards in stack-seeding/isolation/safety_doc_drift_test.go (re-confirmed GREEN).
tests.go_note: Unchanged by M20 — M20 touched only bash + docs. All 4 Go modules still build + pass -count=1 (regression-checked at close). The M15 safety.md drift guards (TestSafetyDocClerkHostsMatchCode / TestSafetyDocDirectusTokenKeysMatchCode / TestSafetyDocForcedBucketOverride / TestSafetyDocNamesRealGuardSymbols) re-run GREEN after the §2.7 edit + the close ref-tag edits — the load-bearing prod-safety verification.
tests.python_note: +22 collected in v1.3b/M20 — the set-dress convergence coverage: dev-stack/tests/test_dev_stack.py class DevSetdress (+12: stack-type plumbing dev|demo, the small-200 vs dev-min default preset, the n=0 guard firing for BOTH types, the atomicity seed-floor after a replay miss, the needval trailing-flag deaths, the non-integer-N reject before any CLI, the provision-recipe-failure-aborts-before-replay, and test_capture_never_runs_even_on_the_cache_miss_degraded_path) + demo-stack/tests/test_frontend_build.py class TestSetdressChainContract (+10: 7 static fences — reuse-the-dev-engine, default-on, non-fatal, offset-DSN, after-migrate-before-verify, capture-never, --force-keeps-the-guard-dev-only — plus 3 behavioural: non-fatal-under-set-e, skipped-when-NO_SETDRESS, and success-passes-clean-threads-the-offset-DSN). dev-stack 38->50, demo-stack collected rises with the chain suite. Trajectory: build +16 -> harden +6 -> close +0 (doc-only close fixes). The 2 milestone-touched suites = 134 passed.
tests.shellcheck: both M20-touched bash scripts shellcheck-CLEAN (-S warning): dev-stack/dev-setdress.sh, demo-stack/up-injected.sh.
tests.py_compile: the 2 M20 test files py_compile CLEAN.
tests.flake_gate: 5/5 clean, sequential, on the 2 milestone-touched suites (dev-stack 50 x5 + demo-stack 84 x5). Deterministic — same pass count every run. 0 flakes. (pytest-randomly not installed; the gate is the sequential-repeat form, which catches shared temp dirs / ports / caches.)

coverage.note: Bash tooling is path-enumerated via the Python harness (subprocess + CLI stubs on PATH; no kcov — matches M16/M17/M18/M19 bash-tooling practice, coverage as candidate-finder not a numeric gate). Harden ran 1 deepening pass + 1 confirmation scan (recorded in progress.md). Behavioral branch coverage on the touched files: dev-setdress.sh every die/branch mapped to a test EXCEPT the env-guarded `need go` (L50, scaffolding); up-injected.sh chain SUCCESS path + offset-DSN threading went static-fence-only -> behaviourally pinned.
coverage.seams_pinned: the stack-type plumbing (dev-N | demo-N prefix + the small-200|dev-min default preset, explicit --seed overrides), the atomicity seed-floor (seed ALWAYS runs after the optional cache-first replay; a replay miss degrades to structural-only-still-logs-in), the n=0-dev guard firing for BOTH stack-types (and --force keeping it a dev-only net so it never blocks a demo), the firewall-abort-before-replay (provision-plan --check-env), and THE prod-safety boundary: capture is NEVER invoked on a bring-up (pinned on both the happy path AND the cache-miss degraded branch) + the resolved offset DSN (5432+OFFSET) actually reaching the engine env (mutation-pinned: mutating the chain DSN to base 5432 fails the behavioural test while the static fence still passes).

prod_safety_invariant_verification.verdict: GREEN — the demo set-dress chain is REPLAY+SEED only; CAPTURE is NEVER run on a bring-up. The chain reuses the M13 dev pass byte-for-byte via --stack-type demo, so every read- and write-side guarantee in safety.md carries over by construction (no demo-specific set-dress code path that could drift).
prod_safety_invariant_verification.capture_never_on_bring_up: pinned by test_capture_is_never_invoked_on_a_bring_up (the chain body: grep of up-injected.sh for `stacksnap capture` + `capture --surface` is empty) AND test_capture_never_runs_even_on_the_cache_miss_degraded_path (the engine's degraded branch — harden's extension of the build's happy-path capture-never pin). Mutation-verified: adding a capture fallback to the cache-miss branch fails the test. Capture is a separate, privileged, operator-confirmed prod READ (documented in the net-new snapshot-cold-start.md).
prod_safety_invariant_verification.per_stack_directus_firewall: the chain firewall-checks the per-stack Directus env (the M10 EnvContract via provision-plan --check-env) BEFORE any replay; a per-stack env resolving to the shared prod Directus hard-aborts the pass before a single row is written. Pinned by test_provision_recipe_failure_aborts_before_replay.
prod_safety_invariant_verification.offset_dsn_isolation: the pass targets THIS demo's offset Postgres (5432+OFFSET) via DEV_SETDRESS_DSN, never the base port 5432 (which would hit the dev stack / a wrong DB). Pinned behaviourally by test_chain_success_passes_clean_and_threads_the_offset_dsn (asserts STUB_DSN=...:25432... for N=2/OFFSET=20000) + test_setdress_targets_this_demos_offset_postgres.
prod_safety_invariant_verification.n0_guard_across_types: dev-setdress.sh's N=0-without-force refusal fires regardless of --stack-type (pinned by test_n0_guard_fires_for_demo_type_too + test_n0_is_refused_without_force). up-injected.sh passes --force so the guard never blocks a real demo (demos start at N>=1); it remains a dev safety net, not weakened by demo mode. Pinned by test_force_keeps_the_engine_guard_from_blocking_a_demo.
prod_safety_invariant_verification.atomicity_floor: the chain always SEEDS after the (cache-first, possibly-skipped) REPLAY — a stack is never catalog-replayed-but-unseeded (which would 403 on every authorized route). A replay cache-miss degrades to a structural-only world that still logs in 200. Pinned by test_demo_seed_is_the_atomicity_floor_after_a_replay_miss + test_cache_miss_is_non_fatal_seed_still_runs. The M17 re-run guards (idempotent TRUNCATE-then-reload replay + idempotent seed COPY) make a partial set-dress repairable by re-running.
prod_safety_invariant_verification.m15_drift_guards: GREEN after the safety.md §2.7 edit AND after the close ref-tag edits — re-run at close, all 4 pass. The §2.7 addition (the demo auto-set-dress chain) did not break any code<->doc drift pin.

delivered.snapshot_cold_start_md: corpus/ops/snapshot-cold-start.md (net-new, ~175 lines, dual-level + code-cited): the one case the cache can't shortcut (a fresh box, empty cache, no safe --dsn) -> the sanctioned DSN-export / restore-a-pg_dump-then---dsn path (Option 1 dump-ingest default; Option 2 primary-read fallback), why the wired postgres MCP is NOT a capture source (#M20-D4: it returns JSON rows, not COPY-format bytes; an adapter would re-serialize COPY text for zero gain), the per-stack-Directus bootstrap->replay->boot note, and how it slots into the auto-set-dress bring-up (replay-only, never capture). Indexed in CLAUDE.md + the demo README; cross-linked from snapshot-spec/db-access/safety; all outbound .md + self-anchor links resolve.
delivered.set_dress_chaining: demo-stack/up-injected.sh — chains dev-stack/dev-setdress.sh via --stack-type demo --force AFTER migrate + BEFORE the M18 verify, default-on + NON-FATAL (a failure WARNS, never aborts a good bring-up), DEMO_NO_SETDRESS=1 escape. Threads the resolved offset DSN (postgres://...@localhost:$((5432+OFFSET))/postgres) as DEV_SETDRESS_DSN. ONE engine, two lifecycles = the dev<->demo convergence by construction (#M20-D1).
delivered.stack_type_aware_engine: dev-stack/dev-setdress.sh — made stack-type-aware (--stack-type dev|demo, default dev for back-compat with M13 call sites): picks the prefix (dev-N | demo-N) + the default preset (dev-min | small-200). All safety preserved byte-for-byte (n=0 guard type-agnostic, prod-Directus firewall, never-capture). The shared ParseStackN grammar already accepted demo-N, so only the prefix + default-preset needed parameterizing.
delivered.atomicity_contract: dev-setdress.sh — the seed ALWAYS runs after the (optional, cache-first) replay (the seed is the FLOOR). A catalog-only stack would 403 on every authorized route; a replay miss degrades to a structural-only world that still logs in. The existing pass already enforced seed-after-replay; M20 makes it explicit (comment + a dedicated atomicity-floor test on both dev + demo) (#M20-D3).
delivered.demo_preset: demo default preset = small-200 (vs dev's dev-min) — demos want a fuller world than dev's ~10-user 'never empty' floor; small-200 (200 users / 3 months) is the lightest demo preset, seeds in <1s, still exercises the role mix. An explicit --seed always overrides (#M20-D2).
delivered.safety_27: corpus/ops/safety.md §2.7 (net-new section) — 'The demo auto-set-dress chain reuses the dev pass — the guarantees carry over (v1.3b M20)': not-a-second-implementation (#M20-D1), replay-only-never-capture, the per-stack Directus firewall gates first, the seeder isolation guard + AssertClean still prove zero pollution, the n=0 guard holds across types, atomicity both-or-neither (#M20-D3). Plus the idempotency.md See-also link.
delivered.recipe_skill_updates: corpus/ops/demo/README.md (the auto-set-dress flow + the small-200 default #M20-D2 + the cold-start callout), corpus/ops/demo/recipe-snapshot-world.md (both dev-up AND demo-up auto-replay now), .claude/skills/demo-up/SKILL.md (auto-set-dressed description + argument-hint --no-setdress + the replay-only safety note), + cross-links in db-access.md / snapshot-spec.md / CLAUDE.md.

review.findings_note: 5 findings: 0 scope, 0 code-quality, 0 adversarial-new, 1 docs (DOC-1), 4 decision-triage. The Phase-7 work: DOC-1 fixed the root CLAUDE.md /demo-up skill-table row (it omitted the M20 auto-set-dress that the /dev-up row advertises — a consistency gap with the v1.3 convergence narrative); 3 already-blended decisions (M20-D1/D2/D3) got their (#M20-DK) ref-tags (D4 was already tagged); the adversarial subsection (5 scenarios, all already test-pinned) recorded in decisions.md. No extensions source changed.
review.scope: GREEN — all 4 overview Scope.In items delivered Fate-1 (set-dress chaining, atomicity, cold-start doc + the resolved ISSUE-13 spike, the small-200 preset); all progress boxes checked; 0 orphan TODO/FIXME in either repo. The Out item (S3 blob bytes + cloud SnapshotStore = DEF-M10-01) stays out -> v1.4, signed, untouched.
review.code_quality: GREEN — dev-setdress.sh + up-injected.sh consistent with M16-M19 conventions (the non-fatal-if-wrapper pattern, the needval value-flag guard, the ^[0-9]+$ N validation before arithmetic, BASH_SOURCE/HERE resolution). The demo chain REUSES the dev engine (no fork). The rc=$? cache-miss-as-warning logic verified correct under set -e (the failed command is the if-condition, so $? in the else holds its exit). shellcheck CLEAN (2 scripts); py_compile CLEAN; bash -n CLEAN. 0 must-fix.
review.docs: 1 finding (DOC-1, fixed) — the root CLAUDE.md /demo-up row now advertises the M20 auto-set-dress (was omitting the parity the /dev-up row claims). All other docs accurate vs the implemented tooling: snapshot-cold-start.md (the capture-source policy, the MCP limitation, the DSN-export path), safety.md §2.7 (the replay-only / firewall / isolation / n0 / atomicity invariants), the demo README/recipe/SKILL. 0 broken xrefs (all outbound + self-anchor links resolve). No new top-level unit beyond snapshot-cold-start.md (indexed in CLAUDE.md + the demo README).
review.tests: GREEN — the 2 M20-touched suites (dev-stack 50 + demo-stack 84 = 134) PASS; all 4 Go modules build + pass -count=1 (unchanged from M19); the M15 safety.md drift guards re-run GREEN after the §2.7 + ref-tag edits. Flake gate 5/5 deterministic on both suites. The harden pass added +6 (the degraded/success/error branches, mutation-pinned) with 0 production changes; the build's logic held under every probe. 0 test gaps.
review.adversarial: Phase 2c — 5 scenarios (a non-numeric N reaching arithmetic [the M18 FINDING-A1 class — already guarded at dev-setdress.sh:71 ^[0-9]+$ before any $((..))]; a trailing value-flag under set -u [needval]; a demo bring-up at N=0 [--force keeps the guard dev-only]; a future demo-only set-dress FORK [the convergence fence]; a stacksnap capture slipping onto the bring-up [the capture-never pin on both happy + degraded branches]) — ALL already test-pinned by the build/harden. No new finding required code changes. Scenarios recorded in decisions.md.
review.decision_triage: M20-D1 (reuse the M13 pass via --stack-type demo) blended into safety.md §2.7 with the (#M20-D1) tag; M20-D2 (small-200 demo default) into demo/README.md with (#M20-D2); M20-D3 (atomicity seed-floor) into safety.md §2.7 with (#M20-D3); M20-D4 (cold-start document-only, MCP not a capture source) was already blended + tagged in snapshot-cold-start.md.
review.deferral_audit: GREEN — knowledge/plan/releases/01.3b-dress-rehearsal/m20-lifecycle-convergence/audit-deferrals/deferral-audit-2026-06-09-m20-close.md. As the FINAL milestone, this re-audit is the release-wide M16->M20 pre-close sweep: M20 added 0 new deferrals (all 4 In-items Fate-1; the ISSUE-13 MCP-adapter spike resolved DOCUMENT-ONLY as #M20-D4, NOT a deferral). The only open item is the inherited cross-release DEF-M10-01 (S3 blobs + cloud store -> v1.4, signed) — UNTOUCHED across v1.3b (a file-level scan of the M16->M20 extensions history found zero SnapshotStore/store.go/S3/blob touches), all 4 aging triggers negative, authority intact. 0 repeat, 0 chronic, 0 aged-out.

completeness_ledger.confirmed_covered_fate2: none — M20 had no Fate-2 routing (it is the closing milestone; everything in scope landed).
completeness_ledger.dropped: none — the ISSUE-13 MCP-paging capture adapter was NOT dropped; it was a SPIKE that resolved document-only (#M20-D4) and shipped the sanctioned DSN-export path in full. A resolved spike, not a cut.

DEF-M10-01 (cloud snapshot store + S3 media blob bytes) is INHERITED from v1.2, signed -> v1.4, orthogonal to M20 (M20 touched the set-dress chain, NOT the snapshot-store/S3 area), not aged out. Unchanged. As the final v1.3b milestone, this is the one signed escape-hatch /developer-kit:close-release will carry forward.

notes: M20 is the 5th + FINAL milestone of v1.3b 'dress rehearsal' — it converges the LIFECYCLE: /demo-up now auto-set-dresses by default (cache-first snapshot replay -> a small-200 light seed) at the bring-up tail, exactly as /dev-up has since M13, by REUSING the very same dev-setdress.sh engine via --stack-type demo. ONE engine, two lifecycles — the convergence by construction, not a second implementation. The load-bearing prod-safety fact (verified at close + test-pinned on both the happy and the cache-miss-degraded branches): the bring-up chain does cache-first REPLAY only and NEVER runs stacksnap capture — capture is a separate, privileged, operator-confirmed prod READ, documented in the net-new corpus/ops/snapshot-cold-start.md (which also settles ISSUE-13: the wired postgres MCP is NOT a capture source, because it returns JSON rows not COPY-format bytes; #M20-D4). The per-stack Directus env is firewall-checked before any replay; the n=0 guard fires across types; the seed is the atomicity floor (a stack is never catalog-replayed-but-unseeded -> 403); the M15 safety.md drift guards re-ran GREEN after the §2.7 edit. Close was a very clean shape: 0 scope / 0 code / 0 adversarial-new / 1 docs (DOC-1: the CLAUDE.md /demo-up row) / 0 tests / 4 decision-triage. Reconciled the dress-rehearsal-m20 tag from the build HEAD e4d2f9b to the final HEAD 51a07cb (the tag trailed the harden commit), re-consuming stack-demo to it. The deferral re-audit, as the final-milestone sweep, confirmed the release-wide M16->M20 state: 0 new deferrals, the one inherited signed DEF-M10-01 -> v1.4 untouched + not aged. Go 736 unchanged, Python 338->360 collected (+22, the set-dress convergence suites), flake 0 (5/5). Next: /developer-kit:close-release v1.3b (the user runs it).
